title: River Camping on the Brahmaputra Sand Bars
description: Between November and April, the Brahmaputra's water level drops to reveal massive sandbars (chars) in the middle of the river. These chars become temporary camping sites — you arrive by boat at sunset, camp overnight, and wake up surrounded by water with nothing on the horizon but river and sky.
why_its_special: The Brahmaputra at this point is 10–15km wide. Camping on a char puts you genuinely in the middle of one of Asia's greatest rivers. There is no road, no village, no noise. Just the river. Migratory birds use the same chars — you'll share the sandbar with skimmers, terns, and sometimes bar-headed geese.
incitetales_angle: People fly to the Maldives for 'surrounded by water.' This is better. And it costs ₹2,000.

season.notes: Chars only exist in dry season. By June the entire riverbed is submerged. November is early — some chars may still be forming. December–February is the sweet spot.

local_intel.how_to_get: Arrange through local boat operators at Fancy Bazar Ghat (Guwahati) or Tezpur riverfront. No walk-in service — requires advance booking 2–3 days ahead.
local_intel.entry_fee: Approx ₹1,500–3,000 per person including boat, tent, basic meals. Varies by operator.
local_intel.permit_notes: No formal permit but inform local authorities if camping near border-sensitive areas.
local_intel.best_time_of_day: Arrive at char by 4pm. Sunset on the river is the main event.
local_intel.duration: Overnight — arrive evening, depart next morning

operator.name: Brahmaputra River Experience (BRE)
operator.contact: Ask at Fancy Bazar Ghat or search locally
operator.notes: Several informal operators run this. Quality varies. Ask specifically for char camping — some will offer just a boat ride. Verify they provide proper tents and life jackets.

what_to_bring: Warm layers — river nights are cold even in December
Camera (tripod if you have one — Milky Way from a char is exceptional)
Earplugs optional — the river is surprisingly loud at night
Any personal medication — you are far from help once on the char
what_most_people_miss: Dawn is better than sunset. Most people photograph sunset and sleep through the 5:30am light. The river turns gold and the birds are most active. Wake up early.
cultural_notes: Char communities (people who live on sandbars year-round) are one of the most vulnerable populations in Assam. Be respectful if you encounter them. Do not treat them as a 'photo opportunity.'
